assertions.reason: Ambiguous routing stayed unresolved until an explicit repository was selected, and an invalid freeform reply did not fall back to any default repository.
evidence: `view-session --session-id session-1 --limit 10` showed only the elicitation `Which repository should I work in for this issue?` after `start-session --issue-id issue-1`.

`prompt-session --session-id session-1 --message "Use whichever repository fits best."` followed by `view-session --search "couldn't match"` produced an error activity instead of selecting a repository.

Shared server log lines 38-43 show `No unique repository matched`, `Repository "Use whichever repository fits best." did not match any pending selection option`, and `Repository selection for agent session session-1 is still unresolved...`.

assertions.reason: Once a repository was selected, the same issue/session continued into session tracking and runner initialization without losing issue state.
evidence: `view-session --session-id session-1 --limit 30` showed the prompt `F1 Frontend Repository` followed by `Repository "F1 Frontend Repository" has been selected by user...` and continued analysis thoughts.

Shared server log lines 43-61 show `User selected repository: F1 Frontend Repository`, then `Tracking session for issue issue-1`, then Gemini runner startup using the issue title/description for classification.

Shared server log lines 195-214 show the same lifecycle continuity for `session-2` / `issue-2`, including `User selected repository: F1 Backend Repository`, `Tracking session for issue issue-2`, and `Building initial prompt for issue DEF-2`.

assertions.reason: After repository selection, the orchestration prompt still enumerated both applicable repositories instead of collapsing the session to a single ambient repository context.
evidence: `view-session --session-id session-2 --limit 20` showed `Selected procedure: **orchestrator-full**` and `Entering 'orchestrator' mode...` after the backend selection reply.
Shared server log lines 255-289 include `<repository_routing_context>` with both `F1 Backend Repository` and `F1 Frontend Repository` under `<available_repositories>`.
Shared server log lines 210-214 show prompt assembly happening after the selected backend repository was applied, while still carrying the multi-repository routing context forward.

assertions.reason: The assertion requires validating post-restart restoration behavior, but this assignment prohibited stopping or restarting the shared F1 service.
evidence: The mission/task constraints explicitly said `Do not stop or restart the service.`

Separate CLI calls (`start-session`, `prompt-session`, `view-session`) preserved live runtime state within the same service process, but no restart-based restore path could be exercised from this surface.

assertions.reason: The zero-association session remained explicit from start through invalid selection handling until a repository was explicitly chosen.
evidence: `start-session --issue-id issue-1` followed by `view-session --session-id session-1 --limit 10` showed only the repository-selection elicitation and no preselected repository.
The invalid reply `Use whichever repository fits best.` produced an error activity and the server log line `Repository selection for agent session session-1 is still unresolved...`.
Log string counts for `Agent session session-1 not found` and `Issue issue-1 not found` were both zero, showing the unresolved state stayed explicit rather than breaking downstream lookups.

assertions.reason: The ambiguous issue moved cleanly into one explicit repository association via exact-name selection and continued downstream without singular shortcut loss.
evidence: `prompt-session --session-id session-1 --message "F1 Frontend Repository"` followed by `view-session --session-id session-1 --limit 30` showed the exact-name selection thought and continued analysis thoughts in the same session.
Shared server log lines 43-61 show the exact-name selection immediately followed by `Tracking session for issue issue-1` and Gemini runner startup using the selected issue/session state.
Log string counts for `Agent session session-1 not found` and `Issue issue-1 not found` were zero.

assertions.reason: The multi-repository flow preserved cross-repository context through routing and orchestration instead of truncating the session to one repository.
evidence: `create-issue` / `assign-issue` / `start-session` for `issue-2` began from the same ambiguous selection state as `issue-1`, proving the workspace still had multiple candidate repositories.

After `prompt-session --session-id session-2 --message "Please use F1 Backend Repository for this issue."`, `view-session --session-id session-2 --limit 20` showed the backend selection plus orchestrator procedure selection in the same session timeline.

Shared server log lines 255-289 preserved both `F1 Backend Repository` and `F1 Frontend Repository` inside `<repository_routing_context>` during prompt assembly, so the multi-repo context was not truncated to one repository.

assertions.reason: Selection-driven association updates propagated consistently into session activity, prompt assembly, and downstream runtime state.
evidence: Session 1 exact-name selection updated the timeline from elicitation -> prompt -> selection thought -> follow-on analysis without changing session or issue identity.
Session 2 natural-language selection updated the timeline from elicitation -> prompt -> selection thought -> `orchestrator-full` procedure -> orchestrator-mode thought.

Shared log evidence showed zero occurrences of `Agent session session-1 not found`, `Issue issue-1 not found`, `Agent session session-2 not found`, and `Issue issue-2 not found`, indicating downstream state remained consistent after selection.

commandsRun.command: rg -n -C 3 "session-1|issue-1|session-2|issue-2|User selected repository|repository_routing_context|orchestrator-full|Entering 'orchestrator' mode|CLAUDE\.local\.md|No such file or directory|optional local prompt" /var/folders/5s/rldbq43s2yj4v5xb89bwygv80000gn/T/droid-bg-1772940152200.out
commandsRun.observation: Confirmed repository-selection, session-tracking, and multi-repository prompt-context evidence in the shared server log.
Verified zero occurrences of avoidable optional-local-prompt noise and zero `Agent session/Issue not found` messages for issue-1/session-1 and issue-2/session-2.

frictions: The shared server log showed unrelated Git/worktree noise (`git fetch origin` publickey warning and invalid branch-name errors for titles containing `::`), but the repository-selection/session-continuation assertions still proceeded and were observable.
`view-session` truncates long activity messages with ellipses, so the shared server log had to be inspected to confirm the full repository-selection and `<repository_routing_context>` details.
blockers: VAL-RUNTIME-004 remained blocked because the assignment prohibited stopping or restarting the shared F1 service, so post-restart runtime-state restoration could not be exercised from this surface.
summary: Validated the F1 multi-repo CLI runtime flow: 7 assigned assertions passed across ambiguous routing, exact-name selection, natural-language selection, downstream continuation, and multi-repository routing context; 1 assertion (VAL-RUNTIME-004) was blocked by the no-restart constraint.
